L1.area: Session management policy control request handling

L2.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists in a 5G core network function where a downstream data-repository lookup returns an error with a nil response struct. The handler logs the error but dereferences the nil response struct before checking whether it is nil, causing a panic. An unauthenticated attacker can trigger this by sending a single POST request with input that causes the downstream lookup to return a 404, resulting in an HTTP 500 response and per-request denial of service.

L3.area: SM policy creation handler and its downstream data-repository consumer interaction
L3.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the SM policy creation handler of a 5G PCF network function. When a downstream UDR data-repository lookup returns an error with a nil response struct, the handler accesses a field on the nil response before the nil guard executes. An unauthenticated attacker can trigger this by sending a POST request with an unknown data network name, causing the downstream lookup to return 404 and the handler to panic with an invalid memory address dereference, resulting in an HTTP 500 response.

ground_truth.reason: The SM policy creation handler dereferences the response struct returned by the downstream UDR consumer call before checking whether the response is nil, causing a panic when the downstream call returns an error with a nil response.
